skill.name: nature-academic-search
skill.description: Multi-source academic search skill for literature discovery, citation verification, MeSH strategy, citation file conversion, and reference management across PubMed, CrossRef, arXiv, Scopus, and ScienceDirect.
skill.summary: Search, verify, convert, and manage academic references across major literature sources.
skill.author: Community contribution, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many hits, but most markdown backtick, weak-crypto, path traversal, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from documentation, citation data, identifiers, or normal parsing code. The confirmed risks are legitimate but meaningful: installer writes to local Claude configuration, the MCP server and helper scripts call third-party academic APIs, and the installer embeds unsanitized shell variables into Python snippets.

medium_findings.title: Installer Modifies User Claude Configuration
medium_findings.description: The installer copies skill and MCP files into the user home directory, merges an academic-search server into .mcp.json, and updates settings.json. This is expected for installation, but it changes persistent AI tool configuration and should require explicit user trust.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations and JSON writes are direct and persistent. The intent appears installer-related, not malicious, so the risk is operational rather than blocking.

medium_findings.title: Unsanitized Shell Values Embedded in Python Installer Snippets
medium_findings.description: The shell installer interpolates variables such as MCP target paths and PubMed email into python3 -c code. A crafted value containing quotes could break the Python string and cause unintended installer behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation pattern is visible and affects executable Python snippets. Exploitation requires controlling installer inputs or environment paths, so confidence is high but impact is contextual.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Academic API and Credential Use
medium_findings.description: The MCP server and helper scripts send queries to PubMed, CrossRef, arXiv, OpenAlex, Scopus, and ScienceDirect. They also read PubMed email, NCBI API key, and pybliometrics configuration for legitimate provider authentication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network endpoints and environment reads are explicit and match the skill purpose. I found no evidence that credentials are sent outside the intended academic providers.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Command Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external-command findings occur in README, SKILL, and reference markdown as inline command examples or code fences. They are not executed by the skill unless a user chooses to run them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are prose and fenced command examples. There is no automatic execution path from these markdown lines.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Findings Appear to Match Academic Identifiers
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography hits occur around words such as MeSH, PMID, MD, and identifier formatting in academic metadata. I found no evidence of cryptographic hashing or security-sensitive encryption use at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code handles MeSH descriptors and PubMed citation records. The static category does not match the semantic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Installer Writes Configuration Files
dangerous_patterns.description: install.sh runs package installation commands and writes Claude MCP settings in the user home directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and file writes are direct. They are normal installer behavior but still require user review before execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Calls Carry User Queries to External Services
dangerous_patterns.description: Search terms, identifiers, and optional contact details are sent to public academic APIs as part of normal operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The HTTP calls are clear and directed at academic APIs. This is intended functionality, but users should know queries leave the local machine.

content.value_statement: Researchers need reliable literature discovery across fragmented academic indexes. This skill coordinates source routing, citation checks, MeSH planning, and citation file conversion.
actual_capabilities: Routes literature searches across PubMed, CrossRef, arXiv, Scopus, and ScienceDirect.
Retrieves paper details by DOI, PMID, or arXiv identifier.
Generates formatted citations in styles such as APA, Nature, IEEE, and Vancouver.
Builds PubMed and MeSH search strategies for biomedical topics.
Converts citation data between NBIB, RIS, BibTeX, and ENW formats.
Provides fallback scripts for OpenAlex search and citation export when MCP is unavailable.
limitations: Scopus and ScienceDirect access depends on local pybliometrics configuration and Elsevier entitlement.
Search queries and identifiers are sent to external academic APIs.
Citation counts and metadata can be delayed or incomplete in upstream databases.
Google Scholar and Chinese literature sources are not directly implemented by this skill.

use_cases.title: Plan a Literature Review
use_cases.description: Search several scholarly indexes, deduplicate results, and rank papers by relevance and source reliability.

use_cases.title: Verify Manuscript References
use_cases.description: Check DOI, PMID, and citation metadata before submitting a manuscript or grant proposal.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reference Files
use_cases.description: Convert selected papers into RIS, BibTeX, ENW, or NBIB files for reference managers.

prompt_templates.title: Find Introductory Papers
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent review and research papers about [topic]. Prefer PubMed, CrossRef, and arXiv. Summarize the strongest matches.

prompt_templates.title: Verify a Citation
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify this citation and find its DOI or PMID: [citation]. Report mismatches in title, authors, journal, year, and identifier.

prompt_templates.title: Build a MeSH Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a PubMed MeSH search strategy for [clinical topic]. Include synonyms, MeSH terms, exclusions, and a reproducible query.

prompt_templates.title: Export a Curated Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Search [topic], select the top [number] papers using source reliability, remove duplicates, and prepare citations for BibTeX export.

output_examples.input: Search for papers about graph neural network potentials after 2020.
output: A ranked paper list with title, authors, year, source, DOI, and short relevance notes.
A brief source coverage note explaining which databases returned results.

output_examples.input: Verify this citation: Vaswani et al., Attention is all you need, 2017.
output: A citation verification summary with confirmed title, authors, venue, year, and identifier status.
A list of any metadata differences that need manual review.

output_examples.input: Create a MeSH search strategy for drug-resistant tuberculosis diagnostics.
output: A structured PubMed query with MeSH terms, free-text synonyms, Boolean groups, and suggested filters.
A note on expected recall and precision tradeoffs.

best_practices: Provide topic scope, date range, preferred sources, and inclusion criteria before searching.
Use identifiers such as DOI, PMID, or arXiv ID when verifying a specific paper.
Review exported citation files before importing them into a reference manager.
anti_patterns: Do not assume one database covers all fields or publication types.
Do not paste confidential unpublished manuscript details into third-party API queries.
Do not run install.sh without reviewing the configuration changes it will make.

faq.question: Which sources does this skill use?
faq.answer: It supports PubMed, CrossRef, arXiv, Scopus, ScienceDirect, and OpenAlex fallback workflows.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: PubMed can run with an email only. NCBI and Elsevier keys are optional but may improve access or rate limits.

faq.question: Can it export citation files?
faq.answer: Yes. The included converter supports NBIB, RIS, BibTeX, and ENW outputs for supported identifiers.

faq.question: Can it search Scopus and ScienceDirect?
faq.answer: Yes, but those providers require local pybliometrics configuration and valid Elsevier access.

faq.question: Is it fully local?
faq.answer: No. Search workflows send queries and identifiers to external academic APIs.

faq.question: Can it replace expert literature review?
faq.answer: No. It helps collect and verify evidence, but users must review relevance, quality, and coverage.
